message:
fix(lifecycle): add lifecycle mutex to resilience monitor, autoscanner, timeline lifecycle/persister, and supervisor to serialize Start/Stop

Continues the systematic concurrency hardening from commit 86a9f4b0,
applying the same lifecycleMu pattern to the remaining 4 subsystems
that had the same class of Start/Stop race condition.

Resilience Monitor:
- lifecycleMu serializes Start() and Stop()
- Stop() clears cancel and done under mu before blocking on done
  channel, eliminating a second lock/unlock cycle after drain

AutoScanner:
- lifecycleMu serializes Start() and Stop()
- Start() takes lifecycleMu first, drops mu quickly after checking
  running, re-acquires mu to set state

Timeline Lifecycle + Persister:
- Both gain lifecycleMu and a stopped bool flag
- Stop() sets stopped = true under lifecycleMu so
  StartSession/EndSession/StartCheckpoint bail out immediately
- TimelineLifecycle.Stop() calls persister.FinalizeSession directly
  instead of recursing through EndSession, avoiding deadlock on
  lifecycleMu

Supervisor:
- lifecycleMu and stopped flag added
- Replaced context.Context field with shutdownCh chan struct{} and
  sync.Once-guarded cancel function, decoupling daemon process
  contexts from supervisor lifecycle
- Start(), Stop(), StopAll(), and Shutdown() all serialize through
  lifecycleMu
- Health checks and daemon monitoring select on shutdownCh instead of
  s.ctx.Done()
- Health HTTP/cmd checks use context.Background() instead of s.ctx so
  they are not prematurely cancelled during shutdown drain

Each subsystem has a corresponding test proving Start() blocks while
a concurrent Stop()/Shutdown() is in progress and correctly resumes
afterward, using channel synchronization with 50ms race windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
